storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: She dances within the embrace of green flames, their dim flickering light falling across the dancer's young, devotion-filled face. With every turn, the emerald fire ignites the souls of the wicked, burning away every impurity upon the stage.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 31@1, December, the police received a report from the manager of the second theater in @2 Garden. The reporter claimed that there had been four small-scale fires in the theater within a week, and rumors of the "Ballet Ghost" were rampant among the members of the dance troupe renting the theater.

To ensure the smooth premiere of the ballet "Swan's Lament," the police launched a joint investigation with the West District Fire Department.

The day before the performance, Mythag University finally took over the investigation. The investigators noted traces of Gnosis residue behind several fire points. Eventually, Investigator @3 discovered an Awakener in the underground ruins of the second theater.

This Awakener was not a mindless individual; she identified herself as "Liz." After communication, the Awakener Liz eventually admitted to her arson crime. After the premiere of "Swan's Lament," Investigator @3 took Liz back to Mythag University for formal incorporation.

According to historical records, when the fire broke out at the second theater in @2 Garden on A.F. 29@1, a ballet was being performed. Among those who perished in the flames were a number of nobles in the audience, as well as a young prima ballerina named "Elizabeth Sur."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Liz is a reliable Awakener; although her attitude cannot be called "enthusiastic", she shows considerable cooperation with all school affairs.

The only concern is that, though Liz spends every night in the dance classroom, no one has seen her true dance performance.

As an excellent ballet dancer, Liz's name has appeared in several related publications. Unfortunately, due to the impact of the D-phenomenon, the information department of the awakening system could only determine that Liz had stepped onto the stage of the second theater in A.F. 29@1.

School reporter Hannah Peasley proposed gathering dance enthusiasts on campus, using their passion for dance to "open Awakener Liz's heart".

Currently, this proposal has yet to be approved by the Awakener Management Office.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Day 45 of joining the dance troupe. I stood on stage as the lead performer.

The scorching lights struck the hem of my skirt, and the tiny diamonds scattered a dazzling brilliance across the air. The glare made it hard to see, but the thunderous applause told me that my performance shone brighter than any gem.

In endless spinning, all the dust of the world is purified by the dance, and the beauty of art is carried out to every corner. This is my wish and my mission as a dancer—

But who are those people lurking in the dark corners beneath the stage?

Lisette, the girl known for her carefree and upright nature—why is she whispering with that notorious lord?

Angelica, the genius who always looks down on the other dancers—why is she lifting her skirt for that infamous playboy?

Such disgrace... such filth! Oh God, my muse, tell me—what must I do to guard my stage from the darkness? What light is needed to dispel this filth—

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
This is the Promised Land, an artistic utopia, the ultimate paradise—dance should be the dancer's home, kept in eternal purity.

So long as I burn, filth cannot taint our Church... This is my mission, my hope... Let me burn brightly, and I shall cleanse away all impurity, for everyone.
